keywords: noise; neighbour; by-law; dispute; after hours
summary: Owner reports repeated late-night noise from neighbouring lot.
original_message: I have been hearing loud music from the unit above me after 10pm for the last three weeks. Nothing has changed after speaking with them.
recommended_action: Log the complaint, check the noise by-law, issue a breach warning if supported, and follow up within 48 hours.
suggested_response: Thank you for raising this with us. We have logged your concern and will review it against the scheme by-laws before confirming the next step. Please keep a record of any further incidents with dates and times.
previous_resolution: By-law notice issued and matter resolved within 7 days.
escalation_rule: Escalate when repeated, hostile, or likely to become a formal dispute.

keywords: water leak; ceiling; pipe; plumber; emergency
summary: Water is leaking from a common area ceiling near the lift.
original_message: There is water dripping from the ceiling on Level 3 near the lift and it is getting worse.
recommended_action: Dispatch emergency plumber, notify building manager, isolate affected water supply if safe, and notify affected residents.
suggested_response: Thank you for alerting us. We are treating this as urgent and arranging contractor attendance. Please avoid the affected area while we coordinate the repair.
previous_resolution: Plumber attended within 45 minutes and replaced failed pipe joint.
escalation_rule: Always escalate same day when water, electrical, safety, or access risk is present.

summary: Owner asks why their levy increased compared with last quarter.
original_message: My quarterly levy has gone up and I do not understand why. Can someone explain the increase?
recommended_action: Check levy schedule and AGM minutes, confirm approved budget or special levy, then send supporting documents.
suggested_response: Thank you for getting in touch. We will review the approved levy schedule and provide the relevant explanation and documents for your property.
previous_resolution: AGM minutes and levy schedule sent; owner satisfied.
escalation_rule: Escalate if levy appears unapproved or account is in dispute.

keywords: proposal; quote; strata management; new building
summary: Prospective client asks for strata management proposal.
original_message: We are looking for a new strata manager for our building and would like to know your fees and process.
recommended_action: Acknowledge enquiry, collect scheme details, send capability statement, and schedule discovery call.
suggested_response: Thank you for contacting us. We would be happy to discuss your strata management needs and prepare next steps after confirming a few building details.
previous_resolution: Discovery call booked and proposal sent within 2 business days.
escalation_rule: Human review optional unless urgent committee deadline is mentioned.

summary: Existing client cannot access the owner portal.
original_message: I cannot log in to the portal and need access to my levy notices.
recommended_action: Verify identity, check portal account status, reset access, and provide instructions.
suggested_response: Thank you for letting us know. We will check your portal access and send reset instructions once your account is verified.
previous_resolution: Portal invitation resent; owner logged in successfully.
escalation_rule: Escalate if personal data or account ownership is unclear.

summary: Client complains about contractor conduct or property damage.
original_message: The contractor was rude to residents and left damage in the common hallway.
recommended_action: Log complaint, request photos/details, notify contractor manager, and arrange inspection if damage is alleged.
suggested_response: Thank you for reporting this. We will log the contractor concern, request supporting details, and follow up with the contractor and building representative.
previous_resolution: Contractor apologised and repaired minor damage within 3 days.
escalation_rule: Escalate to manager when damage, safety, or repeated contractor behaviour is alleged.

summary: Client asks for general clarification about strata by-laws.
original_message: Can you explain whether pets are allowed in a strata building?
recommended_action: Provide general information, clarify that scheme-specific by-laws must be checked, and offer document review.
suggested_response: Thank you for your question. Pet rules depend on the specific scheme by-laws, so we recommend checking the current registered by-laws before taking action.
previous_resolution: General guidance provided; client requested a by-law review.
escalation_rule: Human review optional unless legal dispute or enforcement action is involved.

keywords: lift; elevator; outage; elderly; disabled; access
summary: Lift outage affects vulnerable residents and upper-floor access.
original_message: The lift has been broken for two days and elderly residents cannot use the stairs.
recommended_action: Treat as safety issue, contact lift contractor urgently, notify residents, and arrange welfare checks for vulnerable occupants.
suggested_response: Thank you for raising this. We are treating the lift outage as urgent and will coordinate contractor attendance and resident updates.
previous_resolution: Emergency repair completed within 6 hours; welfare checks completed.
escalation_rule: Always escalate when access, vulnerable residents, or safety risk is present.

summary: Committee member asks for help with meeting agenda or minutes.
original_message: Can you add a motion to the next committee meeting agenda about painting the foyer?
recommended_action: Confirm meeting date, draft agenda motion, check notice requirements, and acknowledge inclusion.
suggested_response: Thank you. We will review the meeting schedule and add the proposed motion if it meets notice and agenda requirements.
previous_resolution: Motion added to agenda and circulated with notice.
escalation_rule: Escalate if motion has legal, budget, or by-law implications.

keywords: arrears; payment plan; hardship; overdue; levy
summary: Owner requests help with overdue levies or hardship.
original_message: I am behind on levies and need to discuss a payment plan.
recommended_action: Check arrears balance, review policy, advise payment plan options, and route to accounts manager.
suggested_response: Thank you for contacting us. We will review your account and payment plan options, then respond with the next steps for approval.
previous_resolution: Payment plan approved and account monitored monthly.
escalation_rule: Escalate to accounts manager for arrears, hardship, or debt recovery matters.
